author: @chimedemon
text:
Dude my first (and currently only) play through I did a pacifist run, and when I got to Asgore, I spent so many hours trying not to kill him being like “JUST KEEP GOING!!! PLEASE, I WANT TO SAVE YOU PLEASE!!!” And it took me hours before I decided looking up how to beat him in a pacifist run was probably a good idea. Realizing that you actually need to nearly kill him in order to progress made me almost broken…

It was one of the first games I felt like I could impact others for the better. I remember as a little kid playing Infamous with my friend and never wanting to harm another person, while he’d just kill everyone. We did that thing where we took turns playing the game every time one of us dies, but it was his game and I was really bad so I’d constantly die… but I was determined to live long enough to make sure people were safe, aaaand yeah… god it was depressing

author: @bigbossnass9240
text: I love the typography joke that Sans is the cool powerful chad that everyone likes and Papyrus is the bumbling bafoon who no one takes seriously lol. And no "Sans" does not mean "Comic Sans" It means a Sans Serif typeface, as in without a serif. A serif is the little like shoe that you'd see at the bottom and top of letters purely as a decorative thing. So like a capital I would have 4 serifs, two on the bottom and two on the top. So Comic Sans would be included in Sans' personality, that's why he's always cracking jokes. But he embodies all sans serif typefaces, like the beloved Helvetica. And that makes him a badass.

text:
This video is 10 years old.
At the time, Scorpion King (referenced in the video) was over a decade old. The CGI used for The Rock, is basically what they boast as “Next Gen graphics” today. If you look at the WWE 2K games, those games’ graphical definition is actually LESS detailed than that of The Scorpion King, done way back for a 2002 release. 

We really haven’t progressed in technology. Just in what they have allowed the public to access.
